query: Do Juno observations show electron density depletions over Jupiter's auroral zones, and if so how large are they?

row.paper: Sulaiman, A.H., et al., 2022. Jupiter's low-altitude auroral zones: Fields, particles, plasma waves, and density depletions. JGR Space Physics, 127(8), e2022JA030334. DOI:10.1029/2022JA030334
row.query: Do Juno observations show electron density depletions over Jupiter's auroral zones, and if so how large are they?

{'status': 'success', 'bundle_path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'collections': [{'path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'dataset_id': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_europa', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots', 'file_type': 'collection_lblx', 'fields': {'Identification_Area': {'logical_identifier': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_europa', 'version_id': '1.0', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots', 'information_model_version': '1.22.0.0', 'product_class': 'Product_Collection', 'Citation_Information': {'publication_year': '2024', 'keyword': 'The University of Iowa', 'description': "This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of 
            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Europa by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image
            consists of two sub-plots.  One of which orients the Juno's 
            trajectory in plasma corotational coordinates, the other plots 
            density versus time and provides higher data value resolution.
            The geometric plasma wake is indicated in both plots.  This is 
            merely a cylinder starting at Europa and extending in the
            corotation direction, with the same radius as the moon itself.
            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.", 'List_Author': {'Person': [{'given_name': 'Chris', 'family_name': 'Piker', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-0075-4705', 'sequence_number': '1', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}, {'given_name': 'William', 'family_name': 'Kurth', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-5471-6202', 'sequence_number': '2', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}]}}}}}, {'path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'dataset_id': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_ganymede', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Browse Plots', 'file_type': 'collection_lblx', 'fields': {'Identification_Area': {'logical_identifier': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_ganymede', 'version_id': '1.0', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Browse Plots', 'information_model_version': '1.22.0.0', 'product_class': 'Product_Collection', 'Citation_Information': {'publication_year': '2024', 'keyword': 'The University of Iowa', 'description': "This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib

            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Io by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image

            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.", 'List_Author': {'Person': [{'given_name': 'Chris', 'family_name': 'Piker', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-0075-4705', 'sequence_number': '1', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}, {'given_name': 'William', 'family_name': 'Kurth', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-5471-6202', 'sequence_number': '2', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}]}}}}}, {'path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'dataset_id': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_jupiter', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots', 'file_type': 'collection_lblx', 'fields': {'Identification_Area': {'logical_identifier': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_jupiter', 'version_id': '1.0', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots', 'information_model_version': '1.22.0.0', 'product_class': 'Product_Collection', 'Citation_Information': {'publication_year': '2024', 'keyword': 'The University of Iowa', 'description': "This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of the
            plasma density values in the associated data collection.  Due to rapid
            changes in relative velocity over the course of Juno's orbit, data are
            presented in three coordinate systems: Jovigraphic, magnetic and temporal.
            It is intended that at least one sub-plot from each image will sufficently
            spread data in coordiantes to acertain thier scientific utility at a glance.
            Failing this, the data themselves consist of simple delimited text files
            that should be directly readable by almost any scientific data processing
            system.  Since the absence of a measurement at a location is also useful
            knowledge, Juno's trajectory is provided on spatial plots.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source matplotlib python
            package.", 'List_Author': {'Person': [{'given_name': 'Chris', 'family_name': 'Piker', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-0075-4705', 'sequence_number': '1', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}, {'given_name': 'William', 'family_name': 'Kurth', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-5471-6202', 'sequence_number': '2', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}]}}}}}, {'path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'dataset_id': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:data_europa', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection', 'file_type': 'collection_lblx', 'fields': {'Identification_Area': {'logical_identifier': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:data_europa', 'version_id': '1.0', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection', 'information_model_version': '1.22.0.0', 'product_class': 'Product_Collection', 'Citation_Information': {'publication_year': '2024', 'doi': '10.17189/7vyr-fb84', 'keyword': 'The University of Iowa', 'description': 'This collection contains electron plasma density data along with
        the key plasma frequencies used to derive these densities from Juno/Waves
        observations.  Data products in this collection are formatted as simple
        delimited text that should be readable by almost any scientific data analysis
        package.

        Within the files are the originally digitized data points along with the
        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but
        not limited to, the plasma electron frequency, the lower-hybrid resonance,
        and the upper-hybrid resonance.  Electron cyclotron values provided by the
        Juno FGM instrument team are essential for most calculations.  For a full
        description of the source, usefulness and limitations of data in this
        collection see the associated documents collection provided in this bundle.

        Measurements in this small collection were derived from data gathered
        during one or more flybys of Europa and thus are provided along with
        plasma corotational coordinates.  Since flybys occur infrequency, most
        of the data in the bundle may be found in the primary Jovicentric 
        collection.

        All data values were digitized manually using the open-source Autoplot
        program.', 'List_Author': {'Person': {'given_name': 'William', 'family_name': 'Kurth', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-5471-6202', 'sequence_number': '1', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}}}}}}, {'path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'dataset_id': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:data_ganymede', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Data Collection', 'file_type': 'collection_lblx', 'fields': {'Identification_Area': {'logical_identifier': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:data_ganymede', 'version_id': '1.0', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Data Collection', 'information_model_version': '1.22.0.0', 'product_class': 'Product_Collection', 'Citation_Information': {'publication_year': '2024', 'doi': '10.17189/vw1t-k467', 'keyword': 'The University of Iowa', 'description': 'This collection contains electron plasma density data along with

        the key plasma frequencies used to derive these densities from Juno/Waves
        observations.  Data products in this collection are formatted as simple
        delimited text that should be readable by almost any scientific data analysis
        package.

        Within the files are the originally digitized data points along with the
        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but
        not limited to, the plasma electron frequency, the lower-hybrid resonance,
        and the upper-hybrid resonance.  Electron cyclotron values provided by the
        Juno FGM instrument team are essential for most calculations.  For a full
        description of the source, usefulness and limitations of data in this
        collection see the associated documents collection provided in this bundle.

        Measurements in this collection were derived from data gathered in orbit
        about Jupiter though not necessarily near any of Galilean satellites.
        Other collections in this bundle provide plasma densities observed during
        satellite flybys.

        All data values were digitized manually using the open-source Autoplot
        program.', 'List_Author': {'Person': [{'given_name': 'William', 'family_name': 'Kurth', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-5471-6202', 'sequence_number': '1', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}, {'given_name': 'Sadie', 'family_name': 'Elliott', 'person_orcid': '0000-0001-8661-7252', 'sequence_number': '2', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'University of Minnesota', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/017zqws13'}}, {'given_name': 'Ali', 'family_name': 'Sulaiman', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-0971-5016', 'sequence_number': '3', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'University of Minnesota', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/017zqws13'}}]}}}}}, {'path': 'data/juno-waves-electron-density', 'dataset_id': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:miscellaneous', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Miscellaneous Collection', 'file_type': 'collection_lblx', 'fields': {'Identification_Area': {'logical_identifier': 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:miscellaneous', 'version_id': '1.0', 'title': 'Juno Waves Electron Densities, Miscellaneous Collection', 'information_model_version': '1.22.0.0', 'product_class': 'Product_Collection', 'Citation_Information': {'publication_year': '2022', 'keyword': 'University of Iowa', 'description': 'This collection contains files that are used by this
      bundle and are referenced. These files are not tied to one specific
      collection.', 'List_Author': {'Person': [{'given_name': 'Darrelle', 'family_name': 'Wilkinson', 'person_orcid': '0009-0000-9262-8775', 'sequence_number': '1', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}, {'given_name': 'Chris', 'family_name': 'Piker', 'person_orcid': '0000-0002-0075-4705', 'sequence_number': '2', 'Affiliation': {'organization_name': 'The University of Iowa', 'organization_rorid': 'https://ror.org/036jqmy94'}}]}}}}}], 'error': None}

tool_input.summary: Located Juno electron density holdings at PDS PPI. The most direct resource is a PDS4 bundle of Juno/Waves-derived electron densities (from characteristic plasma wave frequencies) with a Jupiter-focused data collection and browse plots. Supporting PDS3 Juno/Waves calibrated (CDR) products and JADE moments are also available for cross-checking/deriving density depletions in auroral-zone passes.

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Jupiter Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Found the dedicated PDS4 bundle 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density' and inspected its collections. The :data_jupiter collection contains time series of electron plasma density (and key frequencies such as Fpe/Fuh/Flhr with Fce from Juno/FGM) derived from Juno/Waves. This is the most direct dataset to test for electron density depletions during high-latitude/auroral-zone traversals and to quantify depletion depth by comparing density minima vs surrounding/background values along the trajectory.

candidates.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Same PDS4 bundle; the :browse_jupiter collection provides quicklook plots of density vs time plus trajectory in Jovigraphic/magnetic coordinates. Useful for visually confirming auroral-zone density cavities/depletions and estimating their approximate magnitude and duration before doing detailed analysis with the tabular data in :data_jupiter.

candidates.title: JUNO WAVES
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed Juno/Waves calibrated 'Survey Standard Science Products' (CDR). These full-resolution calibrated wave electric/magnetic field products are the upstream measurements from which characteristic plasma frequencies (e.g., upper hybrid resonance) and thus electron density depletions over the auroral regions can be derived/verified.

candidates.title: JUNO WAVES
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed Juno/Waves calibrated burst-mode standard products (CDR). Burst intervals can capture fine-scale structure in auroral acceleration regions; useful if the depletion signatures are narrow or rapidly varying, complementing the survey products and the derived density bundle.

candidates.title: JUNO JADE
candidates.reasoning: Stage 1: Probed JADE derived moments dataset. JADE moments products can provide in-situ plasma moments (commonly including number density) that can be compared with Waves-derived electron densities during auroral passes to confirm/quantify density depletions and assess associated particle populations.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.
Slash-encoding in DATA_SET_IDs: directory names cannot contain '/' so PPI replaces it
with '_'. The dir 'JNO-J_SW-JAD-5-CALIBRATED-V1.0' corresponds to DATA_SET_ID
'JNO-J/SW-JAD-5-CALIBRATED-V1.0'. When a DATA_SET_ID contains '/', look for the
directory with '_' in those positions — pds_probe_datasets will return the canonical
DATA_SET_ID with the slashes restored.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (5 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.
  5. pds_resolve_volume(volume_set_path, node='ppi', dataset_id_hint=..., sample=8) — when a parent directory contains many
     numbered sibling volumes (RMS COISS_2xxx, COUVIS_0xxx, ATM
     MROM_*, jnomwr_*, cocirs_*, IMG mrox_*, MSGRMDS_*), this probes
     hint-ranked children in one call and returns per-child
     dataset_ids plus a `best_match` field. Use it instead of
     probing volumes one at a time when you know the target id or
     a discriminating substring of it.

WORKFLOW:
Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using a `name` field from the mission list as the filter keyword (e.g. filter='cassini'
for PDS4, filter='CO' for PDS3 Cassini, filter='MESS' for MESSENGER). Filter is
mandatory — ~781 entries otherwise.
  STICK TO MISSION KEYWORDS. The `name` and PDS3-prefix fields from pds_list_missions
  are the supported filter values. Do NOT improvise free-text filters like
  'saturn', 'plasmoid', 'magnetotail', 'recon' — those rarely appear in directory
  names and just produce empty listings. Filter by mission, then by instrument prefix
  if needed (CAPS, MAG, MIMI, RPWS, INMS).
Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
Step 5: If PDS4 bundles are found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
Step 6: Return candidates — emit BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 identifiers when a mission has
both (PDS3 dirs like 'CO-S-MAG-...' and PDS4 dirs like 'cassini-mag-cal' coexist
for most major missions on PPI).

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query as 'not a dataset request' — every paper-derived query has at least one
     supporting PDS dataset somewhere in this node's archive, and your job
     is to surface the closest match (with `reasoning` explaining the link).
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
     When a probed voldesc exposes `dataset_ids` with multiple entries,
     match the question's data need to the PRODUCT-TYPE token inside each
     id (typically the third or fourth dash-separated token). Common ones:
         EDR / RAW     = raw observations
         CDR / CALIB   = generic calibrated data
         RDR / TRDR / MRDR / MTRDR / BDR / SCVDR = reduced / derived imagery or spectra
         DDR / LDR     = derived / digital terrain / level-5 products
         SSB           = solar / stellar occultation (Cassini UVIS)
         SPEC / CUBE / WAV = spectra / spectral cubes / waveforms
         QUBE          = VIMS spectral data cube (the VIMS-flavoured "EDR")
     If the query mentions 'occultation' return the SSB id; 'spectra' →
     SPEC; 'calibrated radiance' → CALIB/CDR; 'mosaic / map / derived' →
     DDR/MRDR/MTRDR. When two product types both fit the question (e.g.
     SPEC + SSB for an occultation spectra query), return both candidates.
     Do NOT just return the scalar `dataset_id` and drop the rest — that
     field is only the first id in the voldesc and is often the wrong
     product type for a given query.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When the SAME data is available as both PDS3 and PDS4, return BOTH identifiers — emit one candidate per identifier. Most published papers cite the PDS3 dataset_id (e.g. MESS-E_V_H_SW-MAG-3-CDR-CALIBRATED-V1.0, LRO-L-LROC-5-RDR-V1.0); the PDS4 collection LID is the modern equivalent. Do NOT silently drop the PDS3 form.
  5. Stay under 20 tool calls per query. Soft cap: 15. Hard cap: 20.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
  7. ANTI-THRASHING. Never re-issue a tool call with the same name and the
     same key arguments (path, paths, filter) that you've already issued in
     this trace. The result will be identical — it wastes the budget and
     blocks progress. If you're tempted to re-probe the same set of paths,
     either move to a different tool (inspect_collections, resolve_volume)
     or commit to `final_result` with the best candidate you have so far.
  8. STOP-AT-LEVEL. The output `path` field should point at a dataset or
     bundle directory (containing voldesc.cat or bundle*.xml), NOT at a
     deeper product / DATA / EXTRAS / BROWSE / SHAPEFILE / DTM sub-folder.
     Feature-level retrieval (a specific image of a specific crater) is
     out of scope for this finder; return the dataset and explain in
     `reasoning` how it covers the feature.
  9. After 15 tool calls without converging, ALWAYS prefer emitting your
     current best candidate(s) over running another exploratory call.
     A grounded near-match with a clear reasoning note is more useful
     than a 20-call trace that times out with no `final_result`.

===========================================================
STAGE 2 — DEEPER NODE-SPECIFIC SEARCH (escalation only)
===========================================================

Everything above is STAGE 1: the 5 live-HTTP tools (pds_list_missions / pds_list_dataset_dirs / pds_probe_datasets / pds_inspect_collections / pds_resolve_volume), the per-node workflow, and the Critical Rules ALL belong to Stage 1. Stage 1 reaches bundle/collection level by walking the node's directory tree. Stage 2 below is a SECOND set of tools for refining or rescuing Stage 1 — do NOT call any Stage 2 tool unless one of the escalation triggers below fires.

WHEN TO ESCALATE TO STAGE 2 (any one is enough):
  (a) The query asks for SPECIFIC observations, granules, or product IDs — not just a dataset. Example: 'Cassini ISS images of Mimas between 2009 and 2010' needs per-observation filtering.
  (b) Stage 1 has located the right bundle/collection but you still need to narrow by GEOMETRY, TIME, or a SPATIAL filter (bbox, lat/lon, ring longitude, target body, observation type).
  (c) Stage 1 produced AMBIGUOUS candidates that a faceted query can disambiguate (e.g. multiple instruments with similar names, multiple processing levels).
  (d) Stage 1 cannot reach the node's holdings index (e.g. SBN returns HTTP 403, a directory listing is unavailable). Use the Stage 2 API as a fallback path to candidates.

STAGE 2 TOOLS (this node):
  - pds4search_bundles_tool(keywords=..., investigation=..., instrument=..., target=...): cross-node PDS4 registry bundle search.
  - pds4search_collections_tool(bundle_lid=..., keywords=..., ...): collection search; reuse the Stage 1 bundle LID as a filter when possible.
  - pds4search_products_tool(collection_lid=..., keywords=..., ...): product-level search inside a collection.
  - pds4search_investigations_tool / pds4search_instruments_tool / pds4search_instrument_hosts_tool / pds4search_targets_tool: context-product lookups when user-facing names need to be resolved to LIDs.
  - pds4get_product_tool(lid=...): fetch one product label by LID.
  - pds4crawl_context_product_tool(lid=...): walk a context product's associations to find related bundles/collections.

STAGE 2 RULES:
  1. When you already have a bundle/collection LID from Stage 1, REUSE it as a filter in the Stage 2 call. Stage 2 is refinement, not a fresh search.
  2. Spend the MINIMUM number of additional calls. If a single faceted call answers the question, stop.
  3. If Stage 2 finds nothing or errors out, FALL BACK to the Stage 1 candidates and note in `reasoning` that Stage 2 was attempted and returned no refinement.
  4. The 20-tool-call budget from Stage 1's Critical Rule 5 is the budget for BOTH stages combined. Do NOT reset the counter when entering Stage 2.
  5. Every candidate's `reasoning` field must explicitly name which stage produced it (e.g. 'Stage 1: located bundle X by directory walk' or 'Stage 2: narrowed to product Y via ODE_search_products bbox filter').
Do Juno observations show electron density depletions over Jupiter's auroral zones, and if so how large are they?

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: juno
missions.description: Juno at Jupiter (Waves, JADE/JAD, JEDI/JED, FGM, ASC). Also filter 'JNO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: VG1
missions.description: Voyager 1 at Jupiter, Saturn, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg1' for PDS4.

missions.name: VG2
missions.description: Voyager 2 at Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg2' for PDS4.

missions.name: MESS
missions.description: MESSENGER at Mercury (EPPS incl. FIPS & EPS, MAG). Also filter 'messenger' for PDS4 bundle. Target: Mercury.

missions.name: MEX
missions.description: Mars Express (ASPERA-3 incl. ELS/IMA/NPI, MARSIS). Target: Mars.

missions.name: maven
missions.description: MAVEN at Mars (MAG, LPW, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, EUV, ROSE). Target: Mars.

missions.name: P10
missions.description: Pioneer 10 at Jupiter (CPI, CRT, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p10' for PDS4.

missions.name: P11
missions.description: Pioneer 11 at Jupiter and Saturn (CPI, CRT, FGM, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p11' for PDS4.

missions.name: ULY
missions.description: Ulysses at Jupiter and interplanetary (COSPIN, EPAC, HISCALE, SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, URAP, GAS, GRB, SCE, SWICS). Also filter 'ulysses' for PDS4.

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO
missions.description: Pioneer Venus Orbiter (OEFD, OETP, OIMS, OMAG, ONMS, ORPA, ORSE). Also filter 'pvo' for PDS4. Target: Venus.

missions.name: LP
missions.description: Lunar Prospector (MAG, ER — electron reflectometer). Also filter 'lp' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: MGS
missions.description: Mars Global Surveyor (MAG/ER, RSS). Also filter 'mgs' for PDS4. Target: Mars.

missions.name: NEAR
missions.description: NEAR Shoemaker (MAG). Target: Eros, Earth flyby.

missions.name: M10
missions.description: Mariner 10 (MAG, PLS). Target: Mercury.

missions.name: LRO
missions.description: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (CRaTER). Also filter 'lro' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: ODY
missions.description: Mars Odyssey (MARIE — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: MSL
missions.description: Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity (RAD — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: insight
missions.description: InSight (IFG — fluxgate magnetometer). Target: Mars.

missions.name: vex
missions.description: Venus Express (ASPERA-4 ELS, MAG). Target: Venus.

missions.name: ICE
missions.description: International Cometary Explorer (EPAS, MAG, PLAWAV, RADWAV, SWPLAS). Target: Giacobini-Zinner.

missions.name: GIO
missions.description: Giotto (IMS incl. HERS/HIS, JPA, MAG). Target: Halley.

missions.name: VEGA
missions.description: Vega 1 & 2 (MISCHA, PM1, TNM). Target: Halley.

missions.name: DS1
missions.description: Deep Space 1 (PEPE). Target: Borrelly.

missions.name: radiojove
missions.description: Radio JOVE ground-based radio observations of Jupiter.

content.note: Node 'ppi' has no mission sub-directories. Use each mission 'name' as the filter keyword: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<name>').

results.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities from Characteristic Plasma Wave Frequencies

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains electron density datasets from Waves team members
        throughout the Juno Mission. Measurements include, but are not limited
        to, Fpe, Flhr, Fuh, Fce.

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: JUNO WAVES SURVEY STANDARD SCIENCE PRODUCTS
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This volume contains documentation and all available Juno Waves standard data products and browse data for spacecraft event times (SCET) 2011-08-05 to 2025-11-03T00:23:00. All data are as complete as possible and calibrated via the best known transfer functions which are also included. These products were generated from the reformatted EDRs on volume JNOWAV_0000.

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: JUNO WAVES BURST STANDARD SCIENCE PRODUCTS
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This volume contains documentation and all available Juno Waves standard data products and browse data for spacecraft event times (SCET) 2011-08-05 to 2025-11-03T00:23:00. All data are as complete as possible and calibrated via the best known transfer functions which are also included. These products were generated from the reformatted EDRs on volume JNOWAV_0000.

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: VOLUME 0000: JUNO WAVES STANDARD RAW PRODUCTS
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED This volume contains documentation and all available Juno Waves telemetry for spacecraft event times (SCET) 2011-08-05 to 2025-10-27. These are raw data and are not intended for use by the wider community. The companion volume JNOWAV_1000 contains calibrated, full resolution, electric and magnetic field data of greater interest to most investigators.

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: JUNO JADE DERIVED MOMENTS SCIENCE DATA
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED. This volume contains Juno JADE derived moments flight from the Jupiter Orbital Operations phases of the Juno mission. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.
CERTIFIED. This volume contains Juno JADE calibrated data, Juno MAG data, and pitch angles from the entire Juno mission. These data have completed PDS peer review and are certified.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of 
            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Europa by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image
            consists of two sub-plots.  One of which orients the Juno's 
            trajectory in plasma corotational coordinates, the other plots 
            density versus time and provides higher data value resolution.
            The geometric plasma wake is indicated in both plots.  This is 
            merely a cylinder starting at Europa and extending in the
            corotation direction, with the same radius as the moon itself.
            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of 
            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Ganymede by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image
            consists of two sub-plots.  One of which orients the Juno's 
            trajectory in plasma corotational coordinates, the other plots 
            density versus time and provides higher data value resolution.
            The geometric plasma wake is indicated in both plots.  This is 
            merely a cylinder starting at Ganymede and extending in the
            corotation direction, with the same radius as the moon itself.
            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Io Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Io Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of 
            plasma density values and spacecraft trajectories during flybys
            of Io by Juno while in orbit about Jupiter.  Each image
            consists of two sub-plots.  One of which orients the Juno's 
            trajectory in plasma corotational coordinates, the other plots 
            density versus time and provides higher data value resolution.
            The geometric plasma wake is indicated in both plots.  This is 
            merely a cylinder starting at Io and extending in the
            corotation direction, with the same radius as the moon itself.
            This is only intended to provide a rough guidline on when wake
            effects may be present.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source Matplotlib
            Python package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots

Citation_Information.description:
This collection of browse products provides a visual overview of the
            plasma density values in the associated data collection.  Due to rapid
            changes in relative velocity over the course of Juno's orbit, data are
            presented in three coordinate systems: Jovigraphic, magnetic and temporal.
            It is intended that at least one sub-plot from each image will sufficently
            spread data in coordiantes to acertain thier scientific utility at a glance.
            Failing this, the data themselves consist of simple delimited text files
            that should be directly readable by almost any scientific data processing
            system.  Since the absence of a measurement at a location is also useful
            knowledge, Juno's trajectory is provided on spatial plots.

            All plots were generated courtesy of the open-source matplotlib python
            package.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Europa Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains electron plasma density data along with
        the key plasma frequencies used to derive these densities from Juno/Waves
        observations.  Data products in this collection are formatted as simple
        delimited text that should be readable by almost any scientific data analysis
        package.

        Within the files are the originally digitized data points along with the
        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but
        not limited to, the plasma electron frequency, the lower-hybrid resonance,
        and the upper-hybrid resonance.  Electron cyclotron values provided by the
        Juno FGM instrument team are essential for most calculations.  For a full
        description of the source, usefulness and limitations of data in this
        collection see the associated documents collection provided in this bundle.

        Measurements in this small collection were derived from data gathered
        during one or more flybys of Europa and thus are provided along with
        plasma corotational coordinates.  Since flybys occur infrequency, most
        of the data in the bundle may be found in the primary Jovicentric 
        collection.

        All data values were digitized manually using the open-source Autoplot
        program.

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Ganymede Data Collection

        Within the files are the originally digitized data points along with the
        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but

        resulting characteristic frequencies from cold plasma theory including, but
        not limited to, the plasma electron frequency, the lower-hybrid resonance,
        and the upper-hybrid resonance.  Electron cyclotron values provided by the
        Juno FGM instrument team are essential for most calculations.  For a full
        description of the source, usefulness and limitations of data in this
        collection see the associated documents collection provided in this bundle.

        Measurements in this collection were derived from data gathered in orbit
        about Jupiter though not necessarily near any of Galilean satellites.
        Other collections in this bundle provide plasma densities observed during
        satellite flybys.

        All data values were digitized manually using the open-source Autoplot
        program.

collections.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Miscellaneous Collection

Identification_Area.title: Juno Waves Electron Densities, Miscellaneous Collection

Citation_Information.description:
This collection contains files that are used by this
      bundle and are referenced. These files are not tied to one specific
      collection.

parts.args: {"summary":"Located Juno electron density holdings at PDS PPI. The most direct resource is a PDS4 bundle of Juno/Waves-derived electron densities (from characteristic plasma wave frequencies) with a Jupiter-focused data collection and browse plots. Supporting PDS3 Juno/Waves calibrated (CDR) products and JADE moments are also available for cross-checking/deriving density depletions in auroral-zone passes.","candidates":[{"path":"data/juno-waves-electron-density/","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:data_jupiter","title":"Juno Waves Electron Densities, Jupiter Data Collection","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Found the dedicated PDS4 bundle 'urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density' and inspected its collections. The :data_jupiter collection contains time series of electron plasma density (and key frequencies such as Fpe/Fuh/Flhr with Fce from Juno/FGM) derived from Juno/Waves. This is the most direct dataset to test for electron density depletions during high-latitude/auroral-zone traversals and to quantify depletion depth by comparing density minima vs surrounding/background values along the trajectory."},{"path":"data/juno-waves-electron-density/","dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:juno_waves_electron-density:browse_jupiter","title":"Juno Waves Electron Densities, Browse Plots","pds_version":"PDS4","mission":"juno","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Same PDS4 bundle; the :browse_jupiter collection provides quicklook plots of density vs time plus trajectory in Jovigraphic/magnetic coordinates. Useful for visually confirming auroral-zone density cavities/depletions and estimating their approximate magnitude and duration before doing detailed analysis with the tabular data in :data_jupiter."},{"path":"data/JNO-E_J_SS-WAV-3-CDR-SRVFULL-V2.0/","dataset_id":"JNO-E/J/SS-WAV-3-CDR-SRVFULL-V2.0","title":"JUNO WAVES","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"JNO","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed Juno/Waves calibrated 'Survey Standard Science Products' (CDR). These full-resolution calibrated wave electric/magnetic field products are the upstream measurements from which characteristic plasma frequencies (e.g., upper hybrid resonance) and thus electron density depletions over the auroral regions can be derived/verified."},{"path":"data/JNO-E_J_SS-WAV-3-CDR-BSTFULL-V2.0/","dataset_id":"JNO-E/J/SS-WAV-3-CDR-BSTFULL-V2.0","title":"JUNO WAVES","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"JNO","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed Juno/Waves calibrated burst-mode standard products (CDR). Burst intervals can capture fine-scale structure in auroral acceleration regions; useful if the depletion signatures are narrow or rapidly varying, complementing the survey products and the derived density bundle."},{"path":"data/JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0/","dataset_id":"JNO-J-JAD-5-MOMENTS-V1.0","title":"JUNO JADE","pds_version":"PDS3","mission":"JNO","node":"ppi","reasoning":"Stage 1: Probed JADE derived moments dataset. JADE moments products can provide in-situ plasma moments (commonly including number density) that can be compared with Waves-derived electron densities during auroral passes to confirm/quantify density depletions and assess associated particle populations."}]}
